basic_details.job_title: HAL Bangalore NATS Apprenticeship Training 2026 – Diploma, BE/BTech & Non-Engineering Graduate Walk-in
basic_details.short_information: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), a Maharatna Central Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of Defence, is conducting a walk-in selection at its Technical Training Institute, Bangalore for one-year NATS Apprenticeship Training under the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS). Three separate drives cover Diploma holders (walk-in on 18 June 2026), BE/BTech Engineering Graduates (walk-in from 22 to 26 June 2026 by stream), and Non-Engineering Graduates (walk-in on 19 June 2026). Reporting time for all drives is 08:00 to 11:00 Hrs.

important_dates.1: BA – Economics / English / Sociology / Public Administration / History / Kannada
important_dates.2: B.Sc. – Physics / Chemistry / Computer Science / Electronics / Electrical / Aviation
important_dates.3: B.Com – Commerce / Banking / Statistics / Accountancy / Finance
important_dates.4: BBA – HR / Marketing / Operations Management / Finance
important_dates.22 june 2026: Aeronautical / Aerospace / Aircraft Maintenance Engineering (AME) Degree
important_dates.23 june 2026: Mechanical / Industrial / Production / Mechatronics / Metallurgy & Material Science
important_dates.24 june 2026: Electrical / Electrical & Electronics / Electronics & Communication / Electronics & Tele-Communication
important_dates.25 june 2026: Computer Engineering / Computer Science & Technology / Computer Science and Engineering / Information Science & Technology / Information Technology

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: 3-year Diploma in Engineering (courses approved by Directorate of Technical Education – DTE) in the relevant stream
BE/BTech degree in the relevant engineering stream from a recognised university in India
BA / B.Sc. / B.Com / BBA / BCA degree in the subject specified in the relevant stream

qualification.details: Eligibility rules are common across all three HAL NATS drives with the key condition being that the qualifying degree or diploma must have been awarded by a recognised university in India on or after 01 August 2021. Candidates who have already undergone, are currently undergoing, or are already registered under a NATS contract elsewhere under the Apprenticeship Act are not eligible. Candidates with work experience of one or more years are also not eligible. | Candidates whose marks are expressed in GPA or CGPA must bring their university's official guidelines for converting GPA/CGPA to percentage. Lateral entry Diploma candidates must produce marks sheets for semesters 3 to 6; regular candidates must produce marks sheets for semesters 1 to 6. For BE/BTech lateral entry candidates, semesters 3 to 8 are required; regular candidates must bring semesters 1 to 8.

salary_details.details: HAL pays a fixed monthly stipend to all NATS apprentices for the 12-month training period. The stipend amount differs by qualification level. Out of the total stipend, 50% is borne by the Government as per existing guidelines. Subsidized canteen facility is also available at the training institute. | Candidates should note that after completion of the apprenticeship training, the contract will be terminated and there is no obligation on the part of HAL to offer any employment. The training is entirely under the NATS framework and carries no promise of absorption into HAL.

selection_process: Selection for HAL NATS Apprenticeship Training 2026 is based on merit on a first-come
first-served basis. There is no written examination. Selection is also subject to vacancy availability at the time of the walk-in.

Candidates report to the venue within the reporting window of 08:00 to 11:00 Hrs on their stream's designated date. Document verification is carried out at the venue. Selection is based on merit and is subject to vacancy.

faq.question: Q1. What is the walk-in date for HAL NATS Apprenticeship 2026 at Bangalore?
faq.answer: There are three separate walk-in drives. Diploma holders must attend on 18 June 2026. Non-Engineering Graduates (BA/BSc/BCom/BBA/BCA) must attend on 19 June 2026. BE/BTech Engineering Graduates have stream-wise dates from 22 to 26 June 2026. Reporting time for all drives is 08:00 to 11:00 Hrs.

faq.question: Q2. Where is the HAL NATS walk-in venue?
faq.answer: The walk-in is held at the Technical Training Institute, Suranjan Das Road, Bangalore – 560017. The contact numbers are 080-22322516 and 080-22323358.

faq.question: Q3. What is the stipend for HAL NATS Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: Diploma holders receive a stipend of Rs. 10,900/- per month. BE/BTech Engineering Graduates and Non-Engineering Graduates (BA/BSc/BCom/BBA/BCA) both receive Rs. 12,300/- per month. The training period is 12 months. Out of the total stipend, 50% is borne by the Government.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for HAL NATS Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Diploma or Degree (BE/BTech or BA/BSc/BCom/BBA/BCA) in the relevant stream from a recognised university in India, obtained on or after 01 August 2021. Candidates with work experience of one or more years are not eligible. Candidates already registered or having completed a NATS contract elsewhere are also not eligible.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for HAL NATS Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in any of the three official notifications for this walk-in selection.

faq.question: Q6. How is selection done for HAL NATS Apprenticeship 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on merit on a first-come, first-served basis and is subject to vacancy availability. There is no written examination. Candidates report to the venue within the reporting window and undergo document verification.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any guarantee of employment after HAL NATS training?
faq.answer: No. After completion of the apprenticeship training, the contract is terminated and HAL has no obligation to offer any employment to the trained apprentice. The engagement is purely for the one-year training period under NATS.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are needed for the HAL NATS walk-in?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring original documents including 10th marks sheet, Degree/Diploma Certificate or Provisional Certificate, all semester mark sheets (as applicable), NATS portal registration printout, two passport-size colour photographs, and one set of self-attested photocopies of all documents. Reserved category candidates must also bring a Caste/PWD Certificate.

faq.question: Q9. What is the NATS portal where I need to register before the walk-in?
faq.answer: Candidates must register as a "STUDENT" at www.mhrdnats.gov.in before attending the walk-in and must bring a printed copy of the registration form to the venue.

faq.question: Q10. What streams are covered under HAL NATS Diploma walk-in on 18 June 2026?
faq.answer: The Diploma walk-in on 18 June 2026 covers Aeronautical/Aerospace/Aviation/Avionics, Aircraft Maintenance Engineering (AME), Mechanical/Industrial/Production/Mechatronics/Metallurgy, Electrical/Electrical & Electronics/Instrumentation, Computer/IT streams, Electronics & Communication, Civil/Chemical Engineering, and Commercial Practice/Library & Information Science.
